channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to oust board members fired by president while case goes on
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Wednesday asked the Supreme Court for an emergency order to oust board members who oversee independent agencies, as a constitutional fight about presidential power plays out.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pauses tariffs on most nations for 90 days, raises taxes on Chinese imports
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125 percent.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: WATCH: Trump’s border czar gets GOP cheers, Democratic tears at Arizona state Capitol
item.description: President Donald Trump's border czar, Tom Homan, was boycotted by Democrat lawmakers at the Arizona legislature within seconds of speaking about the administration's mass deportation program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces 90-day pause on most tariffs, raises taxes on Chinese imports
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-congressman Nickel entering race to unseat North Carolina Sen. Thom Tillis in 2026
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Former Democratic U.S. Rep. Wiley Nickel of North Carolina announced on Wednesday his candidacy to try to unseat Sen.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department suggests US agencies are free to disregard judge's order in law firm dispute
item.description: The Justice Department has suggested to federal agencies that they are free to disregard a judge’s ruling that halted enforcement of a White House executive order that targeted a prominent law firm.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Republicans are going public with their growing worries about Trump's tariffs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Manufacturers struggling to make long-term plans. Farmers facing retaliation from Chinese buyers. U.S. households burdened with higher prices.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Treasury secretary says Trump will keep 10% import tariff, raise import tax on China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump has abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump to fine migrants up to $1K daily for defying deportation order
item.description: The administration is planning to retroactively apply the fines for up to five years, which means some immigrants could owe more than $1 million.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: As cost of new products rises, many thrifty shoppers look to secondhand
item.description: President Trump has said tariffs will counter imbalance in the country’s trade partnerships, but experts have warned this could lead to higher costs for American businesses and consumers — which could mean more people shop secondhand.
item.dc:creator: Alex Haddon, MPR News

item.title: Trump botching tariff messaging because he lacks empathy: Cenk Uygur
item.description: "Why is [Trump] blowing it? Partly because he lacks empathy," Uygur told NewsNation's Leland Vittert on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Texas AG Ken Paxton launches Senate primary challenge against Republican Sen. John Cornyn
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is running for the U.S. Senate in a primary challenge against Republican Sen. John Cornyn, setting up what is likely to become one of the GOP’s most contentious and expensive contests of 2026.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lombardo's crime bill focuses on harsher penalties in Nevada, penalizing repeat offenders
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo is proposing harsher penalties for repeat offenders, DUI drivers and other criminals as part of his sweeping crime bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms Trump’s controversial pick for Pentagon No. 3 job
item.description: The Senate voted Tuesday to confirm Elbridge Colby, President Trump’s “lightning rod” pick to serve as the Pentagon’s under secretary for policy, despite the private concerns of several Republican senators about Colby’s past statements and views.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court lets Trump move forward with firing thousands of federal workers
item.description: The Supreme Court's stay, which allows the administration to execute the firings for now while it litigates in federal court, does not mean the terminations were lawful.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg and Christina Gatti, MPR News

item.title: China vows retaliation and a 'fight to the end' after Trump's latest tariff threat
item.description: Trump said on Truth Social he would impose the new tariffs on China if Beijing did not retract a 34% retaliatory tariff on U.S. goods that it announced in response to Trump's initial salvo last Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch, MPR News

item.title: Jeffries accuses Trump of tanking the economy to create opportunities for the rich
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Monday accused President Trump of intentionally crashing the economy to benefit the GOP’s wealthy donors and other well-heeled interests.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Conservative website‘s bid to access Sen. Mitchell body camera footage revived by appeals court
item.description: Alpha News sought to compel law enforcement to release certain video related to Sen. Nicole Mitchell’s arrest in April 2024. An appeals court ruling has reopened the possibility of body and dash camera footage being released ahead of her trial.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Proposed California bill seeks to extend last call until 4 a.m. in downtown areas
item.description: A new California bill would allow bars and restaurants in designated hospitality zones to serve alcohol until 4 a.m. on certain days, beginning Jan. 1, 2026.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Sen. Champion steps down as ethics committee chair amid conflict-of-interest questions
item.description: Minnesota Senate President Bobby Joe Champion will step down as head of the Subcommittee on Ethics as he faces questions about a possible conflict of interest related to his legal work and funding bills he has sponsored.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump expected to host Netanyahu at White House next week
item.description: President Trump is expected to host Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for a visit on Monday, a White House official told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tampa Bay lawmaker proposes additional Kratom regulations; advocates partially agree
item.description: State lawmakers are looking to add more regulations to the controversial herbal supplement known as Kratom.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: China imposes a 34% tariff on imports of all US products starting April 10
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tariff on imports of all U.S. products beginning April 10, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures following U.S. President Donald Trump’s “Liberation Day” slate of double-digit tariffs. The new tariff matches the rate of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: President Trump’s approval rating slipped to its lowest point during his second White House term amid his handling of the economy and the recent Houthi Signal chat leak, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Pentagon watchdog to review Hegseth’s use of Signal app to convey plans for Houthi strike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon’s acting inspector general announced Thursday that he would review Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s use of the Signal messaging app to convey plans for a military strike against Houthi militants in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Guns, mental health concerns a difficult mix in proposed Nevada law
item.description: Gun rights advocates spoke Wednesday against proposed legislation meant to keep weapons away from people experiencing a mental health crisis. It's a murky area of Nevada law that even makes police uncomfortable.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
